title: Publications
text:
Manuscripts in Preparation & Under Review

Palmeiro, F. M., J. A. Anstey and QBOi co-authors. QBO-resolving model biases and their impacts on the extratropical teleconnections. (In preparation).
Palmeiro, F. M., A. Sanna, P. A. and S. Gualdi. The impact of a better solved stratosphere in the CMCC seasonal prediction system. (In preparation).
Palmeiro, F. M., B. Mezzina, and J. García-Serrano. The ENSO modulation of the 3-dimensional structure of the stratospheric polar vortex. (To be submitted).
Sun, L., F. M. Palmeiro and M. Jucker. Stratosphere Relays El Niño’s Cross-continental Teleconnection from Winter North America to Early-summer Eurasia.(To be submitted)
Blanco-Cabanillas, A., R. K. Scharien, S. E. L. Howell and F. M. Palmeiro. Sea ice regime shifts in Baffin Bay in the context of multi-decadal atmospheric variability. (Under review).
Andrews, M. B., N. Butchart, E. Bednarz, D. Elsbury, F. M. Palmeiro, et al. Extratropical teleconnections in a multi-model ensemble nudged towards the observed QBO. Weather Clim. Dyn. (Under review).
Rodrigo, M., J. L. García-Franco, J. García-Serrano, I. Bladé and F. M. Palmeiro. Mechanisms of the QBO influence on the tropical troposphere: modulation by ENSO conditions. J. Geophys. Res.: Atmos. (Under review).




Published & Peer-Reviewed

Elsbury, D., …, F. M. Palmeiro, et al. (2025). QBOi El Niño Southern Oscillation experiments: Assessing relationships between ENSO, MJO, and QBO. Weather Clim. Dyn.
Rodrigo, M., …, F. M. Palmeiro. (2025). Mechanisms of the QBO influence on the tropical troposphere: climatological SST conditions. J. Geophys. Res.: Atmos.
Naoe, H., …, F. M. Palmeiro, et al. (2025). QBOi El Niño Southern Oscillation experiments: Teleconnections of the QBO. Weather Clim. Dyn.
Kawatani, Y., …, F. M. Palmeiro, et al. (2025). QBOi El Nino Southern Oscillation experiments Part I: Overview of experiment design and ENSO modulation of the QBO. Weather Clim. Dyn.
Mezzina, B., F. M. Palmeiro, H. Goosse, and J. A. Anstey. (2025). Impact of stratospheric polar vortex variability on Antarctic sea ice. Climate Dynamics.
Garfinkel, C. I., …, F. M. Palmeiro, et al. (2025). A process-based evaluation of biases in extratropical stratosphere-troposphere coupling in subseasonal forecast systems. Weather Clim. Dyn.



2024

Volpi, D., J. García-Serrano, F. M. Palmeiro, L. Gil-Reyes, and R. J. Haarsma. (2024). Tropical Atlantic variability in EC-EARTH: impact of the radiative forcing. Climate Dynamics.

Palmeiro, F. M., et al. (2022). Boreal winter stratospheric climatology: CMIP6 version. Climate Dynamics.
Lawrence, Z. D., …, F. M. Palmeiro, et al. (2022). Quantifying stratospheric biases and identifying their potential sources in subseasonal prediction systems. Weather Clim. Dyn.

Palmeiro, F. M., et al. (2017). The complex behaviour of El Niño winter 2015-2016. Geophysical Research Letters.
Palmeiro, F. M., et al. (2015). Comparing Sudden Stratospheric Warming Definitions in Reanalysis Data. Journal of Climate.
Palmeiro, F. M., et al. (2014). Future changes in the Brewer Dobson circulation under different greenhouse gas concentrations. Journal of Atmospheric Sciences.





Reports & Thesis

Palmeiro, F. M. (2018). Sudden Stratospheric Warmings and the Brewer-Dobson Circulation. PhD Thesis, UCM.

title: Froila M. Palmeiro
text:
I completed my PhD in Physics at the Universidad Complutense de Madrid in 2018. My dissertation was entitled Sudden Stratospheric Warmings and the Brewer-Dobson Circulation: diagnostics and interactions. I next expanded my research on stratospheric dynamics and climate modelling at the Barcelona Supercomputing Center and later at the University of Barcelona, where I was also teaching and supervising Master students.
In 2022, I was awarded a Marie Skłodowska-Curie global fellowship to join the Euro-Mediterranean Center on Climate Change (CMCC) in Italy and develop my personal project SD4SP (Stratospheric Dynamics for Seasonal Prediction), to understand and quantify the role of El Niño Southern Oscillation (ENSO) and the Quasi-Biennial Oscillation (QBO) as sources of predictability in seasonal forecasting. I am a long-term visitor at the Canadian Centre for Climate Modelling and Analysis (CCCma) in Victoria, Canada, where the outgoing phase of the project takes place.
My main area of interest is stratosphere-troposphere dynamics, including climate variability and predictability, global circulation patterns and their impacts on the surface as well as their representation in climate models.


SD4SP: Stratospheric Dynamics for Seasonal Prediction 
SD4SP is a personal project funded by the European Commission in the Marie Skłodowska-Curie Actions framework. It aims to disentangle the mechanisms of atmospheric teleconnections via the stratosphere in order to improve seasonal forecasts.
